truth_evidence.fact: H200 numerical kernels should prefer BF16/TF32 when possible (FP32 reduced throughput).

verdict_detail: Dtype-aware noise floors are sensible: bf16 has ~3-decimal mantissa precision (1e-2 floor), fp16 ~3-4 decimals (1e-3), fp32 ~7 decimals (1e-6). Order-of-magnitude alignment with dtype precision.
notes: Floor magnitudes match each dtype's precision character.

truth_evidence.fact: torch.compile works on H200 for prefill-dominant/static-shape graphs; mode=reduce-overhead requires care.
H200 is Hopper architecture, SM_90.

verdict_detail: torch.compile reduce-overhead is documented for Hopper. 20–40% throughput speedup is a projection but not contradicted; the KD training loop is mostly static-shape (max_seq_len=512 padded), so cudagraphs apply.
notes: Speedup magnitude is empirical and unverified, but consistent with Hopper kernel-fusion claims.

truth_evidence.fact: torch.compile works on H200; cudagraph mode reduce-overhead requires static shapes.
Cudagraphs require static input shapes, no CPU-GPU sync inside graph, no in-place ops.

verdict_detail: torch.compile in default/reduce-overhead modes does not introduce numerical approximations (no autotune of reduced-precision kernels). Numerically identical claim is widely accepted.
notes: Caveat: cudagraphs can mask race conditions but do not change numerics.

verdict_detail: Hyperparameter values (lr/bs/grad_accum/seq_len) are paper-derived; H200 truth refs only constrain hardware. See paper crossref 2603.02217#tab1-* for paper consistency.
notes: Cross-checked in paper crossref; bs=8/ga=1 sanctioned by h200.

notes: Minor rounding (15 vs 16 GB headroom) — see also H200-0020/0025; not material.
